title: bursera citronella
intro: Bursera citronella, also known as the Palo de Encino, is a small to medium-sized tree native to Central and South America, belonging to the family Burseraceae. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Bursera citronella include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, and treating skin conditions like eczema and acne. Bursera citronella can be prepared as an herbal tea, essential oil, or tincture to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health ailments.
